1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	################################################################################
3	# Parse config.toml and export CFG_* variables for use by other scripts.
4	#
5	# Usage (source with explicit path):
6	#   source parse_config.sh /path/to/config.toml
7	#
8	# Usage (source with env var):
9	#   CONFIG_TOML=/path/to/config.toml source parse_config.sh
10	#
11	# Usage (source with default - resolves to ../config.toml next to this script):
12	#   source parse_config.sh
13	#
14	# Exported variables (all derived from config.toml - no hardcoding):
15	#
16	#   [repo]
17	#     CFG_LIBNN_ROOT        - libnn source root (absolute)
18	#     CFG_STATE_FILE        - absolute path to state.json
19	#     CFG_WORK_DIR          - absolute path to work directory
20	#
21	#   [toolchain]
22	#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN     - toolchain bin directory
23	#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX  - toolchain prefix (e.g. riscv64-elf-)
24	#     CFG_CC                - full path to gcc                 (derived)
25	#     CFG_AR, CFG_RANLIB    - full paths to ar, ranlib         (derived)
26	#     CFG_OBJDUMP, CFG_GDB  - full paths to objdump, gdb       (derived)
27	#
28	#   [target]
29	#     CFG_CPU               - target CPU name
30	#     CFG_FPGA_CONFIG       - absolute path to .inc hardware file
31	#     CFG_UARCH_REF         - absolute path to uarch doc
32	#     CFG_INTRINSIC_API     - absolute path to intrinsic api doc
33	#     CFG_CFLAGS_BASE       - space-joined base CFLAGS
34	#     CFG_CFLAGS_PERF       - space-joined extra CFLAGS for perf build
35	#     CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY     - space-joined extra CFLAGS for verify build
36	#     CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED   - newline-joined list of defined macros
37	#     CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED - newline-joined list of undefined macros
38	#
39	#   [qemu]
40	#     CFG_QEMU_BIN          - QEMU binary path
41	#     CFG_QEMU_MACHINE      - QEMU machine name (-M argument)
42	#     CFG_QEMU_CPU          - QEMU CPU string (-cpu argument)
43	#     CFG_QEMU_CONFIG       - absolute path to QEMU device config file
44	#     CFG_QEMU_VLEN         - QEMU vector length
45	#     CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT      - QEMU timeout seconds
46	#
47	#   [vsim]
48	#     CFG_VSIM_BIN          - vsim workspace directory
49	#     CFG_VSIM_VLEN         - vsim vector length
50	#     CFG_VSIM_TARGET       - vsim target name (e.g. ax45mpv_premium)
51	#     CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES   - default --log-cycles value
52	#
53	#   [fpga]
54	#     CFG_FPGA_HOST         - FPGA board hostname
55	#     CFG_FPGA_PORT         - FPGA board port
56	#     CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT      - FPGA timeout seconds
57	#     CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT    - absolute path to FPGA linker script
58	#     CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER  - absolute path to GDB wrapper script
59	#     CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG   - absolute path to First_Reset_done flag file
60	#
61	#   [limits]
62	#     CFG_MAX_ROUNDS        - max optimization rounds per function
63	#     CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H  - validate cache TTL in hours
64	################################################################################
65	
66	# Find config file: argument > env var > default (skill-dir/config.toml)
67	_pc_script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
68	_pc_config_path="${1:-${CONFIG_TOML:-${_pc_script_dir}/../config.toml}}"
69	
70	if [ ! -f "${_pc_config_path}" ]; then
71	    echo "parse_config.sh: config.toml not found at ${_pc_config_path}" >&2
72	    return 1 2>/dev/null || exit 1
73	fi

75	eval "$(python3 - "${_pc_config_path}" "${_pc_script_dir}" <<'PYEOF'
76	import sys, tomllib, shlex, os

78	skill_dir   = sys.argv[2]  # directory of parse_config.sh = <skill>/script
79	skill_root  = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(skill_dir, ".."))

81	with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
82	    cfg = tomllib.load(f)
83	
84	repo = cfg.get("repo", {})
85	tc   = cfg.get("toolchain", {})
86	tg   = cfg.get("target", {})
87	qe   = cfg.get("qemu", {})
88	vs   = cfg.get("vsim", {})

91	
92	def q(k, v):
93	    print(f"{k}={shlex.quote(str(v))}")
94	
95	def abspath_rel(base, p):
96	    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
97	    if not p:
98	        return ""
99	    return p if os.path.isabs(p) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, p))
100	
101	def join_flags(section):
102	    """Flatten a [target.cflags_X].flags = [...] list into one string."""
103	    if not isinstance(section, dict):
104	        return ""
105	    flags = section.get("flags", [])
106	    if not isinstance(flags, list):
107	        return ""
108	    return " ".join(str(f) for f in flags)
109	
110	# repo
111	libnn_root = repo.get("libnn_root", "")
112	state_rel  = repo.get("state_file", ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json")
113	work_rel   = repo.get("work_dir",   ".claude/skills/optimize/work")
114	q("CFG_LIBNN_ROOT", libnn_root)
115	q("CFG_STATE_FILE", abspath_rel(libnn_root, state_rel))
116	q("CFG_WORK_DIR",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, work_rel))
117	
118	# toolchain
119	bin_dir = tc.get("bin_dir", "")
120	prefix  = tc.get("prefix", "")
121	q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN",    bin_dir)
122	q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX", prefix)
123	q("CFG_CC",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")     if bin_dir else "")
124	q("CFG_AR",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ar")      if bin_dir else "")
125	q("CFG_RANLIB",  os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ranlib")  if bin_dir else "")
126	q("CFG_OBJDUMP", os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}objdump") if bin_dir else "")
127	q("CFG_GDB",     os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")     if bin_dir else "")
128	
129	# target

131	q("CFG_FPGA_CONFIG",   abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("fpga_config", "")))
132	q("CFG_UARCH_REF",     abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("uarch_ref", "")))
133	q("CFG_INTRINSIC_API", abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("intrinsic_api", "")))
134	q("CFG_CFLAGS_BASE",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_base", {})))

140	q("CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED", "\n".join(ad.get("undefined", [])))

144	q("CFG_QEMU_MACHINE", qe.get("machine", ""))
145	q("CFG_QEMU_CPU",     qe.get("cpu", ""))

147	q("CFG_QEMU_VLEN",    qe.get("vlen", ""))

162	q("CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER", abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("sim_wrapper", "")))

167	q("CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H", lm.get("validate_cache_hours", ""))
168	
169	# For convenience: record the resolved config path itself
170	q("CFG_CONFIG_PATH", config_path)
171	PYEOF
172	)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Parse config.toml and export CFG_* variables for use by other scripts.
#
# Usage (source with explicit path):
#   source parse_config.sh /path/to/config.toml
#
# Usage (source with env var):
#   CONFIG_TOML=/path/to/config.toml source parse_config.sh
#
# Usage (source with default - resolves to ../config.toml next to this script):
#   source parse_config.sh
#
# Exported variables (all derived from config.toml - no hardcoding):
#
#   [repo]
#     CFG_LIBNN_ROOT        - libnn source root (absolute)
#     CFG_STATE_FILE        - absolute path to state.json
#     CFG_WORK_DIR          - absolute path to work directory
#
#   [toolchain]
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN     - toolchain bin directory
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX  - toolchain prefix (e.g. riscv64-elf-)
#     CFG_CC                - full path to gcc                 (derived)
#     CFG_AR, CFG_RANLIB    - full paths to ar, ranlib         (derived)
#     CFG_OBJDUMP, CFG_GDB  - full paths to objdump, gdb       (derived)
#
#   [target]
#     CFG_CPU               - target CPU name
#     CFG_FPGA_CONFIG       - absolute path to .inc hardware file
#     CFG_UARCH_REF         - absolute path to uarch doc
#     CFG_INTRINSIC_API     - absolute path to intrinsic api doc
#     CFG_CFLAGS_BASE       - space-joined base CFLAGS
#     CFG_CFLAGS_PERF       - space-joined extra CFLAGS for perf build
#     CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY     - space-joined extra CFLAGS for verify build
#     CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED   - newline-joined list of defined macros
#     CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED - newline-joined list of undefined macros
#
#   [qemu]
#     CFG_QEMU_BIN          - QEMU binary path
#     CFG_QEMU_MACHINE      - QEMU machine name (-M argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CPU          - QEMU CPU string (-cpu argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CONFIG       - absolute path to QEMU device config file
#     CFG_QEMU_VLEN         - QEMU vector length
#     CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT      - QEMU timeout seconds
#
#   [vsim]
#     CFG_VSIM_BIN          - vsim workspace directory
#     CFG_VSIM_VLEN         - vsim vector length
#     CFG_VSIM_TARGET       - vsim target name (e.g. ax45mpv_premium)
#     CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES   - default --log-cycles value
#
#   [fpga]
#     CFG_FPGA_HOST         - FPGA board hostname
#     CFG_FPGA_PORT         - FPGA board port
#     CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT      - FPGA timeout seconds
#     CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT    - absolute path to FPGA linker script
#     CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER  - absolute path to GDB wrapper script
#     CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG   - absolute path to First_Reset_done flag file
#
#   [limits]
#     CFG_MAX_ROUNDS        - max optimization rounds per function
#     CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H  - validate cache TTL in hours
################################################################################

# Find config file: argument > env var > default (skill-dir/config.toml)
_pc_script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
_pc_config_path="${1:-${CONFIG_TOML:-${_pc_script_dir}/../config.toml}}"

if [ ! -f "${_pc_config_path}" ]; then
    echo "parse_config.sh: config.toml not found at ${_pc_config_path}" >&2
    return 1 2>/dev/null || exit 1
fi

eval "$(python3 - "${_pc_config_path}" "${_pc_script_dir}" <<'PYEOF'
import sys, tomllib, shlex, os
config_path = sys.argv[1]
skill_dir   = sys.argv[2]  # directory of parse_config.sh = <skill>/script
skill_root  = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(skill_dir, ".."))

with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
    cfg = tomllib.load(f)

repo = cfg.get("repo", {})
tc   = cfg.get("toolchain", {})
tg   = cfg.get("target", {})
qe   = cfg.get("qemu", {})
vs   = cfg.get("vsim", {})
fp   = cfg.get("fpga", {})
lm   = cfg.get("limits", {})

def q(k, v):
    print(f"{k}={shlex.quote(str(v))}")

def abspath_rel(base, p):
    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
    if not p:
        return ""
    return p if os.path.isabs(p) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, p))

def join_flags(section):
    """Flatten a [target.cflags_X].flags = [...] list into one string."""
    if not isinstance(section, dict):
        return ""
    flags = section.get("flags", [])
    if not isinstance(flags, list):
        return ""
    return " ".join(str(f) for f in flags)

# repo
libnn_root = repo.get("libnn_root", "")
state_rel  = repo.get("state_file", ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json")
work_rel   = repo.get("work_dir",   ".claude/skills/optimize/work")
q("CFG_LIBNN_ROOT", libnn_root)
q("CFG_STATE_FILE", abspath_rel(libnn_root, state_rel))
q("CFG_WORK_DIR",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, work_rel))

# toolchain
bin_dir = tc.get("bin_dir", "")
prefix  = tc.get("prefix", "")
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN",    bin_dir)
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX", prefix)
q("CFG_CC",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")     if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_AR",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ar")      if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_RANLIB",  os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ranlib")  if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_OBJDUMP", os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}objdump") if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_GDB",     os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")     if bin_dir else "")

# target
q("CFG_CPU",           tg.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_CONFIG",   abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("fpga_config", "")))
q("CFG_UARCH_REF",     abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("uarch_ref", "")))
q("CFG_INTRINSIC_API", abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("intrinsic_api", "")))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_BASE",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_base", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_PERF",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_perf", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY", join_flags(tg.get("cflags_verify", {})))

ad = tg.get("active_defines", {})
q("CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED",   "\n".join(ad.get("defined", [])))
q("CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED", "\n".join(ad.get("undefined", [])))

# qemu
q("CFG_QEMU_BIN",     qe.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_MACHINE", qe.get("machine", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CPU",     qe.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CONFIG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, qe.get("config", "")))
q("CFG_QEMU_VLEN",    qe.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT", qe.get("timeout", ""))

# vsim
q("CFG_VSIM_BIN",        vs.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_VLEN",       vs.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_TARGET",     vs.get("target", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_MODELER",    vs.get("pipeline_modeler", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES", vs.get("log_cycles_default", ""))

# fpga
q("CFG_FPGA_HOST",        fp.get("host", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_PORT",        fp.get("port", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT",     fp.get("timeout", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("linker_script", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER", abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("sim_wrapper", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("reset_flag_file", "")))

# limits
q("CFG_MAX_ROUNDS",       lm.get("max_rounds", ""))
q("CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H", lm.get("validate_cache_hours", ""))

# For convenience: record the resolved config path itself
q("CFG_CONFIG_PATH", config_path)
PYEOF
)"

unset _pc_script_dir _pc_config_path

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Validate config.toml step-by-step. Each step must pass before the next runs."""
3	
4	import os
5	import sys
6	import subprocess
7	import tempfile
8	import tomllib
9	import re
10	import shutil
11	import socket
12	
13	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
14	SKILL_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
15	CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(SKILL_DIR, "config.toml")
16	# Backwards-compat alias: some step functions used OPT_DIR
17	OPT_DIR = SKILL_DIR
18	
19	
20	def _resolve(path, base):
21	    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
22	    if not path:
23	        return ""
24	    return path if os.path.isabs(path) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, path))
25	
26	
27	def libnn_root(cfg):
28	    """Return [repo].libnn_root from config.toml."""
29	    return cfg["repo"]["libnn_root"]
30	
31	GREEN = "\033[92m"
32	RED = "\033[91m"
33	YELLOW = "\033[93m"
34	RESET = "\033[0m"
35	BOLD = "\033[1m"
36	
37	def ok(msg):
38	    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")
39	
40	def fail(msg):
41	    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")
42	
43	def warn(msg):
44	    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")
45	
46	def header(msg):
47	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")
48	
49	def die(msg):
50	    fail(msg)
51	    sys.exit(1)
52	
53	
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	def step_load_config():
58	    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")
59	
60	    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
61	        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")
62	
63	    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")
64	
65	    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
66	        cfg = tomllib.load(f)
67	
68	    required = {
69	        "repo":      ["libnn_root"],
70	        "toolchain": ["bin_dir", "prefix"],
71	        "target":    ["cpu", "fpga_config", "uarch_ref", "intrinsic_api"],
72	        "qemu":      ["binary", "machine", "cpu", "config", "vlen", "timeout"],
73	        "fpga":      ["host", "port", "timeout", "linker_script",
74	                      "sim_wrapper", "reset_flag_file"],
75	        "limits":    ["max_rounds", "validate_cache_hours"],
76	    }
77	    optional = {"vsim"}  # declared in config.toml but not used by /optimize
78	
79	    for section, keys in required.items():
80	        if section not in cfg:
81	            die(f"Missing section [{section}]")
82	        for key in keys:
83	            if key not in cfg[section]:
84	                die(f"Missing key [{section}].{key}")
85	            ok(f"[{section}].{key} = {cfg[section][key]}")
86	
87	    return cfg
88	
89	
90	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# Step 2: Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc exists, Andes flags work
92	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	def step_toolchain(cfg):
94	    header("Step 2: Toolchain")
95	
96	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
97	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
98	
99	    if not os.path.isdir(bin_dir):
100	        fail(f"bin_dir does not exist: {bin_dir}")
101	        die("Fix [toolchain].bin_dir in config.toml and rerun.")
102	
103	    ok(f"bin_dir exists: {bin_dir}")
104	
105	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
106	    if not os.path.isfile(gcc):
107	        die(f"gcc not found: {gcc}")
108	    ok(f"gcc: {gcc}")
109	
110	    # Version
111	    r = subprocess.run([gcc, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
112	    ok(f"version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
113	
114	    # Probe that the toolchain accepts the target flags from config.toml.
115	    # Pull the -mtune/-march/-mabi/-mext-vector flags from [target.cflags_base].
116	    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
117	    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
118	    probe_flags = [f for f in base
119	                   if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=", "-mext-vector"))]
120	    if probe_flags:
121	        r = subprocess.run(
122	            [gcc] + probe_flags + ["-x", "c", "-c", "/dev/null", "-o", "/dev/null"],
123	            capture_output=True, text=True,
124	        )
125	        if r.returncode != 0:
126	            die(f"Target flags not supported: {r.stderr.strip()}")
127	        ok(f"Target flags OK ({' '.join(probe_flags)})")
128	    else:
129	        warn("No -mtune/-march/-mabi/-mext-vector in [target.cflags_base].flags — skipping target flag probe")
130	
131	    # GDB (warn only - needed for FPGA step)
132	    gdb = None
133	    for name in [f"{prefix}gdb-nopython", f"{prefix}gdb"]:
134	        path = os.path.join(bin_dir, name)
135	        if os.path.isfile(path):
136	            gdb = path
137	            break
138	    if gdb:
139	        ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
140	    else:
141	        warn("GDB not in toolchain (will try system GDB for FPGA step)")
142	
143	    return gcc
144	
145	
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	# Step 3: Compile hello world
148	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	HELLO_SRC = '#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n'
150	
151	BUILD_DIR = os.path.join(SKILL_DIR, "build")
152	
153	def step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir):
154	    header("Step 3: Compile hello world")
155	
156	    os.makedirs(BUILD_DIR, exist_ok=True)
157	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello.c")
158	    elf = os.path.join(BUILD_DIR, "hello.adx")
159	
160	    with open(src, "w") as f:
161	        f.write(HELLO_SRC)
162	
163	    # Use a minimal subset of the target CFLAGS to check toolchain acceptance.
164	    # We don't use the full CFG_CFLAGS_BASE because hello world doesn't need
165	    # every target flag; we just need to prove the target flags compile at all.
166	    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
167	    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
168	    verify = (tgt.get("cflags_verify") or {}).get("flags", [])
169	    # Extract only the -mtune/-march/-mext-vector/-mvh style flags
170	    target_flags = [f for f in base + verify
171	                    if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=",
172	                                     "-mext-vector", "-mvh", "-mzfh"))]
173	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src] + target_flags + ["-lm"]
174	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(cmd)}")
175	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
176	    if r.returncode != 0:
177	        die(f"Compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
178	
179	    ok("Compilation succeeded")
180	
181	    r = subprocess.run(["file", elf], capture_output=True, text=True)
182	    if "RISC-V" not in r.stdout:
183	        die(f"Not a RISC-V ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
184	    ok(f"ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
185	
186	    return elf
187	
188	
189	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	# Step 4: QEMU - binary exists, run hello world
191	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
192	def step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=False):
193	    header("Step 4: QEMU")
194	
195	    qemu_bin = cfg["qemu"]["binary"]
196	    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]
197	
198	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_bin):
199	        die(f"QEMU binary not found: {qemu_bin}")
200	    ok(f"Binary: {qemu_bin}")
201	
202	    r = subprocess.run([qemu_bin, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
203	    ok(f"Version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
204	
205	    qemu_cfg = _resolve(cfg["qemu"].get("config", ""), libnn_root(cfg))
206	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_cfg):
207	        die(f"QEMU config not found: {qemu_cfg} (from [qemu].config)")
208	    ok(f"Config: {qemu_cfg}")
209	
210	    machine = cfg["qemu"]["machine"]
211	    cpu_str = cfg["qemu"]["cpu"]
212	
213	    # QEMU semihosting never exits after main() returns - kill after
214	    # enough time for hello world to print.
215	    hello_timeout = 10
216	    qemu_cmd = [
217	        qemu_bin, "-readconfig", qemu_cfg,
218	        "-M", machine,
219	        "-cpu", cpu_str,
220	        "-nographic", "-semihosting", "-bios", elf,
221	    ]
222	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(qemu_cmd)}")
223	
224	    if use_strace:
225	        # LLM sandbox: semihosting output goes to fd 9, not stdout.
226	        # Use strace to capture write syscalls and parse output from there.
227	        strace_log = elf + ".strace"
228	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout),
229	                   "strace", "-f", "-e", "trace=write", "-o", strace_log] + qemu_cmd
230	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, strace, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
231	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
232	
233	        if "HELLO_OK" in (r.stdout or ""):
234	            ok("Output captured via stdout: HELLO_OK")
235	            return
236	
237	        if os.path.isfile(strace_log):
238	            with open(strace_log) as f:
239	                content = f.read()
240	            if "HELLO_OK" in content:
241	                ok("Output captured via strace (semihosting fd): HELLO_OK")
242	                return
243	
244	        die("HELLO_OK not found in QEMU output (checked stdout + strace)")
245	    else:
246	        # Semihosting writes directly to the terminal, not through stdout.
247	        # Let output flow to the user's terminal; verify QEMU didn't crash.
248	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout)] + qemu_cmd
249	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
250	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
251	
252	        if r.returncode == 124:
253	            # timeout killed QEMU - expected, semihosting never exits
254	            ok("QEMU ran until timeout (check terminal for HELLO_OK)")
255	            return
256	        elif r.returncode == 0:
257	            ok("QEMU exited cleanly")
258	            return
259	        else:
260	            die(f"QEMU crashed (rc={r.returncode})")
261	
262	
263	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
264	# Step 5: FPGA .inc - parse hardware features
265	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
266	def step_fpga_config(cfg):
267	    header("Step 5: FPGA .inc config")
268	
269	    path = cfg["target"]["fpga_config"]
270	    if not os.path.isabs(path):
271	        path = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, path)
272	
273	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
274	        die(f"FPGA config not found: {path}")
275	    ok(f"File: {path}")
276	
277	    with open(path) as f:
278	        content = f.read()
279	
280	    int_keys = {
281	        "NDS_VLEN": "vlen",
282	        "NDS_DLEN": "dlen",
283	        "NDS_ELEN": "elen",
284	        "NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB": "dcache_kb",
285	        "NDS_DCACHE_WAY": "dcache_way",
286	        "NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB": "icache_kb",
287	        "NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE": "cache_line",
288	        "NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB": "l2_kb",
289	        "NDS_L2C_WAY": "l2_way",
290	        "NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH": "l2_data_width",
291	        "NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH": "biu_data_width",
292	    }
293	
294	    # Boolean defines use "yes"/"no" strings or _INT suffix with 0/1
295	    bool_keys = {
296	        "NDS_RVV_SUPPORT": "rvv",
297	        "NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT": "vdot",
298	        "NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT": "int4_vload",
299	        "NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT": "bf16",
300	        "NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT": "qmac",
301	    }
302	
303	    features = {}
304	    for vkey, jkey in int_keys.items():
305	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+(\d+)', content)
306	        if m:
307	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1))
308	            ok(f"{vkey} = {m.group(1)}")
309	        else:
310	            warn(f"{vkey} not found")
311	
312	    for vkey, jkey in bool_keys.items():
313	        # Try _INT variant first (numeric), then "yes"/"no" string
314	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}_INT\s+(\d+)', content)
315	        if m:
316	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1)) != 0
317	        else:
318	            m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+"(yes|no)"', content)
319	            if m:
320	                features[jkey] = m.group(1) == "yes"
321	            else:
322	                features[jkey] = False
323	
324	        status = "enabled" if features[jkey] else "disabled"
325	        ok(f"{vkey} = {status}")
326	
327	    return features
328	
329	
330	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
331	# Step 6: FPGA board connectivity
332	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
333	def step_fpga_connectivity(cfg):
334	    header("Step 6: FPGA board connectivity")
335	
336	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
337	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
338	
339	    try:
340	        sock = socket.create_connection((host, port), timeout=5)
341	        sock.close()
342	        ok(f"TCP {host}:{port} open")
343	    except (socket.timeout, ConnectionRefusedError, OSError) as e:
344	        die(f"Cannot connect to {host}:{port}: {e}")
345	
346	
347	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
348	# Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA board
349	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
350	def step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir):
351	    header("Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA")
352	
353	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
354	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
355	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
356	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
357	    lib_root = libnn_root(cfg)
358	
359	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
360	    ld_script = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("linker_script", ""), lib_root)
361	    sim_wrapper = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("sim_wrapper", ""), lib_root)
362	
363	    if not os.path.isfile(sim_wrapper):
364	        die(f"sim-wrapper not found: {sim_wrapper}")
365	
366	    # Find a working GDB. Prefer toolchain gdb to match the toolchain.
367	    gdb = None
368	    for candidate in [
369	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"),
370	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb"),
371	    ]:
372	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
373	            # Quick check: can it run at all?
374	            r = subprocess.run([candidate, "--version"],
375	                               capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
376	            if r.returncode == 0:
377	                gdb = candidate
378	                break
379	    if not gdb:
380	        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
381	    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
382	
383	    # Compile with FPGA linker script
384	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
385	    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
386	    with open(src, "w") as f:
387	        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')
388	
389	    # Reuse the same minimal target-flag subset used in step_compile.
390	    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
391	    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
392	    verify = (tgt.get("cflags_verify") or {}).get("flags", [])
393	    target_flags = [f for f in base + verify
394	                    if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=",
395	                                     "-mext-vector", "-mvh", "-mzfh"))]
396	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src] + target_flags
397	    if os.path.isfile(ld_script):
398	        cmd.append(f"-T{ld_script}")
399	    cmd.append("-lm")
400	
401	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
402	    if r.returncode != 0:
403	        die(f"FPGA compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
404	    ok("Compiled for FPGA")
405	
406	    # Remove the first-reset flag so the wrapper re-resets the board
407	    first_reset = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("reset_flag_file", ""), lib_root)
408	    if first_reset and os.path.isfile(first_reset):
409	        os.remove(first_reset)
410	
411	    env = os.environ.copy()
412	    env["MYPC"] = host
413	    env["MYPORT"] = str(port)
414	    env["PATH"] = f"{bin_dir}:{env.get('PATH', '')}"
415	
416	    # The expect script hardcodes riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython.
417	    # If missing, create a symlink in tmpdir and prepend to PATH.
418	    gdb_nopy = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
419	    if not os.path.isfile(gdb_nopy):
420	        link = os.path.join(tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
421	        os.symlink(gdb, link)
422	        env["PATH"] = f"{tmpdir}:{env['PATH']}"
423	        ok(f"Symlinked {prefix}gdb-nopython -> {os.path.basename(gdb)}")
424	
425	    # The sim-wrapper expects to be run from its own project directory
426	    # (it looks for peer files like gdb-ice-riscv64.exp). Use the dir of
427	    # sim_wrapper as cwd — no hardcoded 'project/ax45mpv'.
428	    wrapper_dir = os.path.dirname(sim_wrapper)
429	    ok(f"cmd: {sim_wrapper} {elf}")
430	    ok(f"  -> cwd {wrapper_dir}")
431	    ok(f"  -> target remote {host}:{port}")
432	    ok(f"Running on FPGA ({host}:{port}, timeout=120s) ...")
433	    try:
434	        r = subprocess.run(
435	            [sim_wrapper, elf],
436	            capture_output=True, text=True,
437	            timeout=120, env=env,
438	            cwd=wrapper_dir,
439	        )
440	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
441	        die("FPGA run timed out (120s)")
442	
443	    combined = (r.stdout or "") + (r.stderr or "")
444	    if "HELLO_FPGA_OK" in combined:
445	        ok("FPGA output: HELLO_FPGA_OK")
446	        return
447	
448	    if r.returncode == 0:
449	        ok(f"FPGA returned 0 (GDB output may not be captured)")
450	        warn(f"stdout tail: {(r.stdout or '')[-200:]}")
451	        return
452	
453	    die(f"FPGA run failed (rc={r.returncode})\n  stdout: {(r.stdout or '')[-300:]}\n  stderr: {(r.stderr or '')[-300:]}")
454	
455	
456	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
457	# Main - sequential, fail-fast
458	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
459	def main():
460	    import argparse
461	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate config.toml step-by-step")
462	    parser.add_argument("--strace", action="store_true",
463	                        help="Use strace to capture QEMU semihosting output (for LLM sandbox)")
464	    args = parser.parse_args()
465	
466	    print(f"{BOLD}config.toml validation - sequential fail-fast{RESET}")
467	    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")
468	
469	    cfg = step_load_config()
470	    print(f"libnn:  {libnn_root(cfg)}")
471	    gcc = step_toolchain(cfg)
472	
473	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="validate_") as tmpdir:
474	        elf = step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir)
475	        step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=args.strace)
476	        step_fpga_config(cfg)
477	        step_fpga_connectivity(cfg)
478	        step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir)
479	
480	    header("All steps passed")
481	
482	
483	if __name__ == "__main__":
484	    main()
485

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Validate config.toml step-by-step. Each step must pass before the next runs."""

import os
import sys
import subprocess
import tempfile
import tomllib
import re
import shutil
import socket

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SKILL_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(SKILL_DIR, "config.toml")
# Backwards-compat alias: some step functions used OPT_DIR
OPT_DIR = SKILL_DIR


def _resolve(path, base):
    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
    if not path:
        return ""
    return path if os.path.isabs(path) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, path))


def libnn_root(cfg):
    """Return [repo].libnn_root from config.toml."""
    return cfg["repo"]["libnn_root"]

GREEN = "\033[92m"
RED = "\033[91m"
YELLOW = "\033[93m"
RESET = "\033[0m"
BOLD = "\033[1m"

def ok(msg):
    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")

def fail(msg):
    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")

def warn(msg):
    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")

def header(msg):
    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")

def die(msg):
    fail(msg)
    sys.exit(1)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_load_config():
    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")

    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")

    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")

    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
        cfg = tomllib.load(f)

    required = {
        "repo":      ["libnn_root"],
        "toolchain": ["bin_dir", "prefix"],
        "target":    ["cpu", "fpga_config", "uarch_ref", "intrinsic_api"],
        "qemu":      ["binary", "machine", "cpu", "config", "vlen", "timeout"],
        "fpga":      ["host", "port", "timeout", "linker_script",
                      "sim_wrapper", "reset_flag_file"],
        "limits":    ["max_rounds", "validate_cache_hours"],
    }
    optional = {"vsim"}  # declared in config.toml but not used by /optimize

    for section, keys in required.items():
        if section not in cfg:
            die(f"Missing section [{section}]")
        for key in keys:
            if key not in cfg[section]:
                die(f"Missing key [{section}].{key}")
            ok(f"[{section}].{key} = {cfg[section][key]}")

    return cfg


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc exists, Andes flags work
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_toolchain(cfg):
    header("Step 2: Toolchain")

    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]

    if not os.path.isdir(bin_dir):
        fail(f"bin_dir does not exist: {bin_dir}")
        die("Fix [toolchain].bin_dir in config.toml and rerun.")

    ok(f"bin_dir exists: {bin_dir}")

    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
    if not os.path.isfile(gcc):
        die(f"gcc not found: {gcc}")
    ok(f"gcc: {gcc}")

    # Version
    r = subprocess.run([gcc, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
    ok(f"version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")

    # Probe that the toolchain accepts the target flags from config.toml.
    # Pull the -mtune/-march/-mabi/-mext-vector flags from [target.cflags_base].
    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
    probe_flags = [f for f in base
                   if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=", "-mext-vector"))]
    if probe_flags:
        r = subprocess.run(
            [gcc] + probe_flags + ["-x", "c", "-c", "/dev/null", "-o", "/dev/null"],
            capture_output=True, text=True,
        )
        if r.returncode != 0:
            die(f"Target flags not supported: {r.stderr.strip()}")
        ok(f"Target flags OK ({' '.join(probe_flags)})")
    else:
        warn("No -mtune/-march/-mabi/-mext-vector in [target.cflags_base].flags — skipping target flag probe")

    # GDB (warn only - needed for FPGA step)
    gdb = None
    for name in [f"{prefix}gdb-nopython", f"{prefix}gdb"]:
        path = os.path.join(bin_dir, name)
        if os.path.isfile(path):
            gdb = path
            break
    if gdb:
        ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
    else:
        warn("GDB not in toolchain (will try system GDB for FPGA step)")

    return gcc


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Compile hello world
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
HELLO_SRC = '#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n'

BUILD_DIR = os.path.join(SKILL_DIR, "build")

def step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir):
    header("Step 3: Compile hello world")

    os.makedirs(BUILD_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello.c")
    elf = os.path.join(BUILD_DIR, "hello.adx")

    with open(src, "w") as f:
        f.write(HELLO_SRC)

    # Use a minimal subset of the target CFLAGS to check toolchain acceptance.
    # We don't use the full CFG_CFLAGS_BASE because hello world doesn't need
    # every target flag; we just need to prove the target flags compile at all.
    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
    verify = (tgt.get("cflags_verify") or {}).get("flags", [])
    # Extract only the -mtune/-march/-mext-vector/-mvh style flags
    target_flags = [f for f in base + verify
                    if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=",
                                     "-mext-vector", "-mvh", "-mzfh"))]
    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src] + target_flags + ["-lm"]
    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(cmd)}")
    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if r.returncode != 0:
        die(f"Compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")

    ok("Compilation succeeded")

    r = subprocess.run(["file", elf], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if "RISC-V" not in r.stdout:
        die(f"Not a RISC-V ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
    ok(f"ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")

    return elf


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: QEMU - binary exists, run hello world
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=False):
    header("Step 4: QEMU")

    qemu_bin = cfg["qemu"]["binary"]
    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]

    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_bin):
        die(f"QEMU binary not found: {qemu_bin}")
    ok(f"Binary: {qemu_bin}")

    r = subprocess.run([qemu_bin, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
    ok(f"Version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")

    qemu_cfg = _resolve(cfg["qemu"].get("config", ""), libnn_root(cfg))
    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_cfg):
        die(f"QEMU config not found: {qemu_cfg} (from [qemu].config)")
    ok(f"Config: {qemu_cfg}")

    machine = cfg["qemu"]["machine"]
    cpu_str = cfg["qemu"]["cpu"]

    # QEMU semihosting never exits after main() returns - kill after
    # enough time for hello world to print.
    hello_timeout = 10
    qemu_cmd = [
        qemu_bin, "-readconfig", qemu_cfg,
        "-M", machine,
        "-cpu", cpu_str,
        "-nographic", "-semihosting", "-bios", elf,
    ]
    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(qemu_cmd)}")

    if use_strace:
        # LLM sandbox: semihosting output goes to fd 9, not stdout.
        # Use strace to capture write syscalls and parse output from there.
        strace_log = elf + ".strace"
        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout),
                   "strace", "-f", "-e", "trace=write", "-o", strace_log] + qemu_cmd
        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, strace, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)

        if "HELLO_OK" in (r.stdout or ""):
            ok("Output captured via stdout: HELLO_OK")
            return

        if os.path.isfile(strace_log):
            with open(strace_log) as f:
                content = f.read()
            if "HELLO_OK" in content:
                ok("Output captured via strace (semihosting fd): HELLO_OK")
                return

        die("HELLO_OK not found in QEMU output (checked stdout + strace)")
    else:
        # Semihosting writes directly to the terminal, not through stdout.
        # Let output flow to the user's terminal; verify QEMU didn't crash.
        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout)] + qemu_cmd
        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)

        if r.returncode == 124:
            # timeout killed QEMU - expected, semihosting never exits
            ok("QEMU ran until timeout (check terminal for HELLO_OK)")
            return
        elif r.returncode == 0:
            ok("QEMU exited cleanly")
            return
        else:
            die(f"QEMU crashed (rc={r.returncode})")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: FPGA .inc - parse hardware features
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_fpga_config(cfg):
    header("Step 5: FPGA .inc config")

    path = cfg["target"]["fpga_config"]
    if not os.path.isabs(path):
        path = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, path)

    if not os.path.isfile(path):
        die(f"FPGA config not found: {path}")
    ok(f"File: {path}")

    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()

    int_keys = {
        "NDS_VLEN": "vlen",
        "NDS_DLEN": "dlen",
        "NDS_ELEN": "elen",
        "NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB": "dcache_kb",
        "NDS_DCACHE_WAY": "dcache_way",
        "NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB": "icache_kb",
        "NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE": "cache_line",
        "NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB": "l2_kb",
        "NDS_L2C_WAY": "l2_way",
        "NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH": "l2_data_width",
        "NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH": "biu_data_width",
    }

    # Boolean defines use "yes"/"no" strings or _INT suffix with 0/1
    bool_keys = {
        "NDS_RVV_SUPPORT": "rvv",
        "NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT": "vdot",
        "NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT": "int4_vload",
        "NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT": "bf16",
        "NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT": "qmac",
    }

    features = {}
    for vkey, jkey in int_keys.items():
        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+(\d+)', content)
        if m:
            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1))
            ok(f"{vkey} = {m.group(1)}")
        else:
            warn(f"{vkey} not found")

    for vkey, jkey in bool_keys.items():
        # Try _INT variant first (numeric), then "yes"/"no" string
        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}_INT\s+(\d+)', content)
        if m:
            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1)) != 0
        else:
            m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+"(yes|no)"', content)
            if m:
                features[jkey] = m.group(1) == "yes"
            else:
                features[jkey] = False

        status = "enabled" if features[jkey] else "disabled"
        ok(f"{vkey} = {status}")

    return features


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: FPGA board connectivity
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_fpga_connectivity(cfg):
    header("Step 6: FPGA board connectivity")

    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]

    try:
        sock = socket.create_connection((host, port), timeout=5)
        sock.close()
        ok(f"TCP {host}:{port} open")
    except (socket.timeout, ConnectionRefusedError, OSError) as e:
        die(f"Cannot connect to {host}:{port}: {e}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA board
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir):
    header("Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA")

    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
    lib_root = libnn_root(cfg)

    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
    ld_script = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("linker_script", ""), lib_root)
    sim_wrapper = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("sim_wrapper", ""), lib_root)

    if not os.path.isfile(sim_wrapper):
        die(f"sim-wrapper not found: {sim_wrapper}")

    # Find a working GDB. Prefer toolchain gdb to match the toolchain.
    gdb = None
    for candidate in [
        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"),
        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb"),
    ]:
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            # Quick check: can it run at all?
            r = subprocess.run([candidate, "--version"],
                               capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
            if r.returncode == 0:
                gdb = candidate
                break
    if not gdb:
        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")

    # Compile with FPGA linker script
    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
    with open(src, "w") as f:
        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')

    # Reuse the same minimal target-flag subset used in step_compile.
    tgt = cfg.get("target", {})
    base = (tgt.get("cflags_base") or {}).get("flags", [])
    verify = (tgt.get("cflags_verify") or {}).get("flags", [])
    target_flags = [f for f in base + verify
                    if f.startswith(("-mtune=", "-march=", "-mabi=",
                                     "-mext-vector", "-mvh", "-mzfh"))]
    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src] + target_flags
    if os.path.isfile(ld_script):
        cmd.append(f"-T{ld_script}")
    cmd.append("-lm")

    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if r.returncode != 0:
        die(f"FPGA compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
    ok("Compiled for FPGA")

    # Remove the first-reset flag so the wrapper re-resets the board
    first_reset = _resolve(cfg["fpga"].get("reset_flag_file", ""), lib_root)
    if first_reset and os.path.isfile(first_reset):
        os.remove(first_reset)

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["MYPC"] = host
    env["MYPORT"] = str(port)
    env["PATH"] = f"{bin_dir}:{env.get('PATH', '')}"

    # The expect script hardcodes riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython.
    # If missing, create a symlink in tmpdir and prepend to PATH.
    gdb_nopy = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
    if not os.path.isfile(gdb_nopy):
        link = os.path.join(tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
        os.symlink(gdb, link)
        env["PATH"] = f"{tmpdir}:{env['PATH']}"
        ok(f"Symlinked {prefix}gdb-nopython -> {os.path.basename(gdb)}")

    # The sim-wrapper expects to be run from its own project directory
    # (it looks for peer files like gdb-ice-riscv64.exp). Use the dir of
    # sim_wrapper as cwd — no hardcoded 'project/ax45mpv'.
    wrapper_dir = os.path.dirname(sim_wrapper)
    ok(f"cmd: {sim_wrapper} {elf}")
    ok(f"  -> cwd {wrapper_dir}")
    ok(f"  -> target remote {host}:{port}")
    ok(f"Running on FPGA ({host}:{port}, timeout=120s) ...")
    try:
        r = subprocess.run(
            [sim_wrapper, elf],
            capture_output=True, text=True,
            timeout=120, env=env,
            cwd=wrapper_dir,
        )
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        die("FPGA run timed out (120s)")

    combined = (r.stdout or "") + (r.stderr or "")
    if "HELLO_FPGA_OK" in combined:
        ok("FPGA output: HELLO_FPGA_OK")
        return

    if r.returncode == 0:
        ok(f"FPGA returned 0 (GDB output may not be captured)")
        warn(f"stdout tail: {(r.stdout or '')[-200:]}")
        return

    die(f"FPGA run failed (rc={r.returncode})\n  stdout: {(r.stdout or '')[-300:]}\n  stderr: {(r.stderr or '')[-300:]}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main - sequential, fail-fast
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def main():
    import argparse
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate config.toml step-by-step")
    parser.add_argument("--strace", action="store_true",
                        help="Use strace to capture QEMU semihosting output (for LLM sandbox)")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    print(f"{BOLD}config.toml validation - sequential fail-fast{RESET}")
    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")

    cfg = step_load_config()
    print(f"libnn:  {libnn_root(cfg)}")
    gcc = step_toolchain(cfg)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="validate_") as tmpdir:
        elf = step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir)
        step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=args.strace)
        step_fpga_config(cfg)
        step_fpga_connectivity(cfg)
        step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir)

    header("All steps passed")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# /optimize skill configuration - template
#
# Copy this to config.toml and edit for your target. The skill NEVER reads
# literal paths, flags, VLENs, hostnames, or CPU names from SKILL.md or from
# scripts under script/. Everything flows through this file.
#
# Adding a new VPU configuration:
#   1. Drop the Andes Verilog config file under fpga_config/
#   2. If the microarchitecture family is new, add references/uarch/<soc>.md
#   3. Edit [target] to point at the new .inc and uarch doc
#   4. Adjust [target.cflags_base] and [qemu].cpu / [qemu].vlen / [vsim].vlen
#
# No SKILL.md or script edits should ever be needed for a new target.

################################################################################
# [repo] - where libnn source lives and where FSM state is persisted
################################################################################
[repo]
# Absolute path to the libnn source tree (build.sh, Source/, Include/, etc.)
libnn_root = "/home/nick/work/libnn_0421"

# State file location, relative to libnn_root (skill writes FSM state here)
state_file = ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json"

# Work directory for intermediate artifacts (compiled ELFs, objdumps, logs),
# relative to libnn_root
work_dir = ".claude/skills/optimize/work"


################################################################################
# [toolchain] - cross-compiler
################################################################################
[toolchain]
bin_dir = "/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
prefix  = "riscv64-elf-"


################################################################################
# [target] - which VPU, its hardware profile, and its build flags
################################################################################
[target]
# Target CPU name (passed to build.sh)
cpu = "AX45MPV"

# Andes Verilog config file (parsed for VLEN, caches, ISA feature flags).
# Relative to the skill directory (.claude/skills/optimize/).
fpga_config = "fpga_config/ax45mpv_v1024_8mb.inc"

# Microarchitecture reference doc (pipeline model, stall taxonomy).
# Proposer subagent reads this during `analyze`. Relative to skill dir.
uarch_ref = "references/uarch/andes_45_series.md"

# Intrinsic API reference doc (which macros are legal in new RVV code).
# Implement subagent reads this. Relative to skill dir.
intrinsic_api = "references/intrinsic_api/nds_vec_macros.md"


# CFLAGS assembled from three sources (order preserved, concatenated):
#   cflags_base    - baseline compile flags (both verify and perf builds)
#   cflags_perf    - extra flags for FPGA perf build (adds PF_COUNTER, etc.)
#   cflags_verify  - extra flags for QEMU correctness build

[target.cflags_base]
# Flags extracted from build.sh's AX45MPV block
flags = [
  "-O3", "-ffunction-sections", "-fdata-sections", "-Wall", "-Werror",
  "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-fno-strict-aliasing",
  "-mext-vector", "-DENA_VEC_ISA",
  "-DMAX_VLEN=1024", "-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000",
  "-DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD",
  "-fno-tree-slp-vectorize", "-fno-tree-vectorize",
]

[target.cflags_perf]
flags = [
  "-DPF_COUNTER", "-DENA_TEST_PERF", "-DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1",
  "-DENA_RUN_TWICE", "-DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP",
]

[target.cflags_verify]
flags = [
  "-DENA_FAST_ALGO", "-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0", "-DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0",
  "-mext-vector=zve64d",
]


[target.active_defines]
# Macros analyze/implement subagents treat as defined or undefined for this
# build (governs which #ifdef branches are live source vs dead code).
defined   = ["ENA_VEC_ISA", "ENA_TILING", "ENA_FAST_ALGO",
             "ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"]
undefined = ["ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC", "ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVQMAC"]


################################################################################
# [qemu] - correctness simulator (ground truth: accuracy only, not perf)
################################################################################
[qemu]
binary  = "/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
machine = "andes_ae350"
cpu     = "andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
# QEMU device config, relative to libnn_root
config  = "project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
vlen    = 1024
timeout = 300


################################################################################
# [vsim] - pipeline simulator (ground truth: pipeline stalls, not perf)
################################################################################
[vsim]
binary             = "/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim"
# Pipeline patterns are VLEN-invariant, so vsim can run at a smaller VLEN for
# speed. Element counts per instruction will differ from the FPGA run, but
# stall shapes are preserved.
vlen               = 512
target             = "ax45mpv_premium"
# Python module under ${[vsim].binary}/tools that exposes a PipelineModeler
# class. Differs per uarch family (e.g. andes45, andes27).
pipeline_modeler   = "andes45"
log_cycles_default = 2000


################################################################################
# [fpga] - ground truth for cycle counts
################################################################################
[fpga]
host    = "sw-boards.andestech.com"
port    = 1119
timeout = 300

# Linker script + sim-wrapper + reset flag, all relative to libnn_root
linker_script   = "project/ax45mpv/ae350-xip_hvm.ld"
sim_wrapper     = "project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
reset_flag_file = "project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"


################################################################################
# [limits] - FSM exit conditions and cache TTLs
################################################################################
[limits]
# Max optimization rounds per function
max_rounds = 10
# Re-validation cache: skip validate state if config + .inc unchanged and
# last validation was more recent than this (hours). 0 = always re-validate.
validate_cache_hours = 24

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. toolUseResult.originalFile:
# /optimize skill configuration - template
#
# Copy this to config.toml and edit for your target. The skill NEVER reads
# literal paths, flags, VLENs, hostnames, or CPU names from SKILL.md or from
# scripts under script/. Everything flows through this file.
#
# Adding a new VPU configuration:
#   1. Drop the Andes Verilog config file under fpga_config/
#   2. If the microarchitecture family is new, add references/uarch/<soc>.md
#   3. Edit [target] to point at the new .inc and uarch doc
#   4. Adjust [target.cflags_base] and [qemu].cpu / [qemu].vlen / [vsim].vlen
#
# No SKILL.md or script edits should ever be needed for a new target.

################################################################################
# [repo] - where libnn source lives and where FSM state is persisted
################################################################################
[repo]
# Absolute path to the libnn source tree (build.sh, Source/, Include/, etc.)
libnn_root = "/home/nick/work/libnn_0421"

# State file location, relative to libnn_root (skill writes FSM state here)
state_file = ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json"

# Work directory for intermediate artifacts (compiled ELFs, objdumps, logs),
# relative to libnn_root
work_dir = ".claude/skills/optimize/work"


################################################################################
# [toolchain] - cross-compiler
################################################################################
[toolchain]
bin_dir = "/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
prefix  = "riscv64-elf-"


################################################################################
# [target] - which VPU, its hardware profile, and its build flags
################################################################################
[target]
# Target CPU name (passed to build.sh)
cpu = "AX45MPV"

# Andes Verilog config file (parsed for VLEN, caches, ISA feature flags).
# Relative to the skill directory (.claude/skills/optimize/).
fpga_config = "fpga_config/ax45mpv_v1024_8mb.inc"

# Microarchitecture reference doc (pipeline model, stall taxonomy).
# Proposer subagent reads this during `analyze`. Relative to skill dir.
uarch_ref = "references/uarch/andes_45_series.md"

# Intrinsic API reference doc (which macros are legal in new RVV code).
# Implement subagent reads this. Relative to skill dir.
intrinsic_api = "references/intrinsic_api/nds_vec_macros.md"


# CFLAGS assembled from three sources (order preserved, concatenated):
#   cflags_base    - baseline compile flags (both verify and perf builds)
#   cflags_perf    - extra flags for FPGA perf build (adds PF_COUNTER, etc.)
#   cflags_verify  - extra flags for QEMU correctness build

[target.cflags_base]
# Flags extracted from build.sh's AX45MPV block
flags = [
  "-O3", "-ffunction-sections", "-fdata-sections", "-Wall", "-Werror",
  "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-fno-strict-aliasing",
  "-mext-vector", "-DENA_VEC_ISA",
  "-DMAX_VLEN=1024", "-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000",
  "-DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD",
  "-fno-tree-slp-vectorize", "-fno-tree-vectorize",
]

[target.cflags_perf]
flags = [
  "-DPF_COUNTER", "-DENA_TEST_PERF", "-DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1",
  "-DENA_RUN_TWICE", "-DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP",
]

[target.cflags_verify]
flags = [
  "-DENA_FAST_ALGO", "-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0", "-DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0",
  "-mext-vector=zve64d",
]


[target.active_defines]
# Macros analyze/implement subagents treat as defined or undefined for this
# build (governs which #ifdef branches are live source vs dead code).
defined   = ["ENA_VEC_ISA", "ENA_TILING", "ENA_FAST_ALGO",
             "ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"]
undefined = ["ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC", "ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVQMAC"]


################################################################################
# [qemu] - correctness simulator (ground truth: accuracy only, not perf)
################################################################################
[qemu]
binary  = "/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
machine = "andes_ae350"
cpu     = "andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
# QEMU device config, relative to libnn_root
config  = "project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
vlen    = 1024
timeout = 300


################################################################################
# [vsim] - pipeline simulator (ground truth: pipeline stalls, not perf)
################################################################################
[vsim]
binary             = "/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim"
# Pipeline patterns are VLEN-invariant, so vsim can run at a smaller VLEN for
# speed. Element counts per instruction will differ from the FPGA run, but
# stall shapes are preserved.
vlen               = 512
target             = "ax45mpv_premium"
# Python module under ${[vsim].binary}/tools that exposes a PipelineModeler
# class. Differs per uarch family (e.g. andes45, andes27).
pipeline_modeler   = "andes45"
log_cycles_default = 2000


################################################################################
# [fpga] - ground truth for cycle counts
################################################################################
[fpga]
host    = "sw-boards.andestech.com"
port    = 1119
timeout = 300

# Linker script + sim-wrapper + reset flag, all relative to libnn_root
linker_script   = "project/ax45mpv/ae350-xip_hvm.ld"
sim_wrapper     = "project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
reset_flag_file = "project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"


################################################################################
# [limits] - FSM exit conditions and cache TTLs
################################################################################
[limits]
# Max optimization rounds per function
max_rounds = 5
# Re-validation cache: skip validate state if config + .inc unchanged and
# last validation was more recent than this (hours). 0 = always re-validate.
validate_cache_hours = 24

# No SKILL.md or script edits should ever be needed for a new target.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Parse config.toml and export CFG_* variables for use by other scripts.
#
# Usage (source with explicit path):
#   source parse_config.sh /path/to/config.toml
#
# Usage (source with env var):
#   CONFIG_TOML=/path/to/config.toml source parse_config.sh
#
# Usage (source with default - resolves to ../config.toml next to this script):
#   source parse_config.sh
#
# Exported variables (all derived from config.toml - no hardcoding):
#
#   [repo]
#     CFG_LIBNN_ROOT        - libnn source root (absolute)
#     CFG_STATE_FILE        - absolute path to state.json
#     CFG_WORK_DIR          - absolute path to work directory
#
#   [toolchain]
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN     - toolchain bin directory
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX  - toolchain prefix (e.g. riscv64-elf-)
#     CFG_CC                - full path to gcc                 (derived)
#     CFG_AR, CFG_RANLIB    - full paths to ar, ranlib         (derived)
#     CFG_OBJDUMP, CFG_GDB  - full paths to objdump, gdb       (derived)
#
#   [target]
#     CFG_CPU               - target CPU name
#     CFG_FPGA_CONFIG       - absolute path to .inc hardware file
#     CFG_UARCH_REF         - absolute path to uarch doc
#     CFG_INTRINSIC_API     - absolute path to intrinsic api doc
#     CFG_CFLAGS_BASE       - space-joined base CFLAGS
#     CFG_CFLAGS_PERF       - space-joined extra CFLAGS for perf build
#     CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY     - space-joined extra CFLAGS for verify build
#     CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED   - newline-joined list of defined macros
#     CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED - newline-joined list of undefined macros
#
#   [qemu]
#     CFG_QEMU_BIN          - QEMU binary path
#     CFG_QEMU_MACHINE      - QEMU machine name (-M argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CPU          - QEMU CPU string (-cpu argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CONFIG       - absolute path to QEMU device config file
#     CFG_QEMU_VLEN         - QEMU vector length
#     CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT      - QEMU timeout seconds
#
#   [vsim]
#     CFG_VSIM_BIN          - vsim workspace directory
#     CFG_VSIM_VLEN         - vsim vector length
#     CFG_VSIM_TARGET       - vsim target name (e.g. ax45mpv_premium)
#     CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES   - default --log-cycles value
#
#   [fpga]
#     CFG_FPGA_HOST         - FPGA board hostname
#     CFG_FPGA_PORT         - FPGA board port
#     CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT      - FPGA timeout seconds
#     CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT    - absolute path to FPGA linker script
#     CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER  - absolute path to GDB wrapper script
#     CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG   - absolute path to First_Reset_done flag file
#
#   [limits]
#     CFG_MAX_ROUNDS        - max optimization rounds per function
#     CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H  - validate cache TTL in hours
################################################################################

# Find config file: argument > env var > default (skill-dir/config.toml)
_pc_script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
_pc_config_path="${1:-${CONFIG_TOML:-${_pc_script_dir}/../config.toml}}"

if [ ! -f "${_pc_config_path}" ]; then
    echo "parse_config.sh: config.toml not found at ${_pc_config_path}" >&2
    return 1 2>/dev/null || exit 1
fi

eval "$(python3 - "${_pc_config_path}" "${_pc_script_dir}" <<'PYEOF'
import sys, tomllib, shlex, os
config_path = sys.argv[1]
skill_dir   = sys.argv[2]  # directory of parse_config.sh = <skill>/script
skill_root  = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(skill_dir, ".."))

with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
    cfg = tomllib.load(f)

repo = cfg.get("repo", {})
tc   = cfg.get("toolchain", {})
tg   = cfg.get("target", {})
qe   = cfg.get("qemu", {})
vs   = cfg.get("vsim", {})
fp   = cfg.get("fpga", {})
lm   = cfg.get("limits", {})

def q(k, v):
    print(f"{k}={shlex.quote(str(v))}")

def abspath_rel(base, p):
    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
    if not p:
        return ""
    return p if os.path.isabs(p) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, p))

def join_flags(section):
    """Flatten a [target.cflags_X].flags = [...] list into one string."""
    if not isinstance(section, dict):
        return ""
    flags = section.get("flags", [])
    if not isinstance(flags, list):
        return ""
    return " ".join(str(f) for f in flags)

# repo
libnn_root = repo.get("libnn_root", "")
state_rel  = repo.get("state_file", ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json")
work_rel   = repo.get("work_dir",   ".claude/skills/optimize/work")
q("CFG_LIBNN_ROOT", libnn_root)
q("CFG_STATE_FILE", abspath_rel(libnn_root, state_rel))
q("CFG_WORK_DIR",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, work_rel))

# toolchain
bin_dir = tc.get("bin_dir", "")
prefix  = tc.get("prefix", "")
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN",    bin_dir)
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX", prefix)
q("CFG_CC",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")     if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_AR",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ar")      if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_RANLIB",  os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ranlib")  if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_OBJDUMP", os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}objdump") if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_GDB",     os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")     if bin_dir else "")

# target
q("CFG_CPU",           tg.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_CONFIG",   abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("fpga_config", "")))
q("CFG_UARCH_REF",     abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("uarch_ref", "")))
q("CFG_INTRINSIC_API", abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("intrinsic_api", "")))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_BASE",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_base", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_PERF",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_perf", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY", join_flags(tg.get("cflags_verify", {})))

ad = tg.get("active_defines", {})
q("CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED",   "\n".join(ad.get("defined", [])))
q("CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED", "\n".join(ad.get("undefined", [])))

# qemu
q("CFG_QEMU_BIN",     qe.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_MACHINE", qe.get("machine", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CPU",     qe.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CONFIG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, qe.get("config", "")))
q("CFG_QEMU_VLEN",    qe.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT", qe.get("timeout", ""))

# vsim
q("CFG_VSIM_BIN",        vs.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_VLEN",       vs.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_TARGET",     vs.get("target", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_MODELER",    vs.get("pipeline_modeler", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES", vs.get("log_cycles_default", ""))

# fpga
q("CFG_FPGA_HOST",        fp.get("host", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_PORT",        fp.get("port", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT",     fp.get("timeout", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("linker_script", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER", abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("sim_wrapper", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("reset_flag_file", "")))

# limits
q("CFG_MAX_ROUNDS",       lm.get("max_rounds", ""))
q("CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H", lm.get("validate_cache_hours", ""))

# For convenience: record the resolved config path itself
q("CFG_CONFIG_PATH", config_path)
PYEOF
)"

unset _pc_script_dir _pc_config_path

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Parse config.toml and export CFG_* variables for use by other scripts.
#
# Usage (source with explicit path):
#   source parse_config.sh /path/to/config.toml
#
# Usage (source with env var):
#   CONFIG_TOML=/path/to/config.toml source parse_config.sh
#
# Usage (source with default - resolves to ../config.toml next to this script):
#   source parse_config.sh
#
# Exported variables (all derived from config.toml - no hardcoding):
#
#   [repo]
#     CFG_LIBNN_ROOT        - libnn source root (absolute)
#     CFG_STATE_FILE        - absolute path to state.json
#     CFG_WORK_DIR          - absolute path to work directory
#
#   [toolchain]
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN     - toolchain bin directory
#     CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX  - toolchain prefix (e.g. riscv64-elf-)
#     CFG_CC                - full path to gcc                 (derived)
#     CFG_AR, CFG_RANLIB    - full paths to ar, ranlib         (derived)
#     CFG_OBJDUMP, CFG_GDB  - full paths to objdump, gdb       (derived)
#
#   [target]
#     CFG_CPU               - target CPU name
#     CFG_FPGA_CONFIG       - absolute path to .inc hardware file
#     CFG_UARCH_REF         - absolute path to uarch doc
#     CFG_INTRINSIC_API     - absolute path to intrinsic api doc
#     CFG_CFLAGS_BASE       - space-joined base CFLAGS
#     CFG_CFLAGS_PERF       - space-joined extra CFLAGS for perf build
#     CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY     - space-joined extra CFLAGS for verify build
#     CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED   - newline-joined list of defined macros
#     CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED - newline-joined list of undefined macros
#
#   [qemu]
#     CFG_QEMU_BIN          - QEMU binary path
#     CFG_QEMU_MACHINE      - QEMU machine name (-M argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CPU          - QEMU CPU string (-cpu argument)
#     CFG_QEMU_CONFIG       - absolute path to QEMU device config file
#     CFG_QEMU_VLEN         - QEMU vector length
#     CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT      - QEMU timeout seconds
#
#   [fpga]
#     CFG_FPGA_HOST         - FPGA board hostname
#     CFG_FPGA_PORT         - FPGA board port
#     CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT      - FPGA timeout seconds
#     CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT    - absolute path to FPGA linker script
#     CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER  - absolute path to GDB wrapper script
#     CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG   - absolute path to First_Reset_done flag file
#
#   [limits]
#     CFG_MAX_ROUNDS        - max optimization rounds per function
#     CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H  - validate cache TTL in hours
################################################################################

# Find config file: argument > env var > default (skill-dir/config.toml)
_pc_script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
_pc_config_path="${1:-${CONFIG_TOML:-${_pc_script_dir}/../config.toml}}"

if [ ! -f "${_pc_config_path}" ]; then
    echo "parse_config.sh: config.toml not found at ${_pc_config_path}" >&2
    return 1 2>/dev/null || exit 1
fi

eval "$(python3 - "${_pc_config_path}" "${_pc_script_dir}" <<'PYEOF'
import sys, tomllib, shlex, os
config_path = sys.argv[1]
skill_dir   = sys.argv[2]  # directory of parse_config.sh = <skill>/script
skill_root  = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(skill_dir, ".."))

with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
    cfg = tomllib.load(f)

repo = cfg.get("repo", {})
tc   = cfg.get("toolchain", {})
tg   = cfg.get("target", {})
qe   = cfg.get("qemu", {})
vs   = cfg.get("vsim", {})
fp   = cfg.get("fpga", {})
lm   = cfg.get("limits", {})

def q(k, v):
    print(f"{k}={shlex.quote(str(v))}")

def abspath_rel(base, p):
    """Return absolute path: p as-is if absolute, else joined with base."""
    if not p:
        return ""
    return p if os.path.isabs(p) else os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base, p))

def join_flags(section):
    """Flatten a [target.cflags_X].flags = [...] list into one string."""
    if not isinstance(section, dict):
        return ""
    flags = section.get("flags", [])
    if not isinstance(flags, list):
        return ""
    return " ".join(str(f) for f in flags)

# repo
libnn_root = repo.get("libnn_root", "")
state_rel  = repo.get("state_file", ".claude/skills/optimize/state.json")
work_rel   = repo.get("work_dir",   ".claude/skills/optimize/work")
q("CFG_LIBNN_ROOT", libnn_root)
q("CFG_STATE_FILE", abspath_rel(libnn_root, state_rel))
q("CFG_WORK_DIR",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, work_rel))

# toolchain
bin_dir = tc.get("bin_dir", "")
prefix  = tc.get("prefix", "")
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN",    bin_dir)
q("CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX", prefix)
q("CFG_CC",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")     if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_AR",      os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ar")      if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_RANLIB",  os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}ranlib")  if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_OBJDUMP", os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}objdump") if bin_dir else "")
q("CFG_GDB",     os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")     if bin_dir else "")

# target
q("CFG_CPU",           tg.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_CONFIG",   abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("fpga_config", "")))
q("CFG_UARCH_REF",     abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("uarch_ref", "")))
q("CFG_INTRINSIC_API", abspath_rel(skill_root, tg.get("intrinsic_api", "")))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_BASE",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_base", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_PERF",   join_flags(tg.get("cflags_perf", {})))
q("CFG_CFLAGS_VERIFY", join_flags(tg.get("cflags_verify", {})))

ad = tg.get("active_defines", {})
q("CFG_DEFINES_DEFINED",   "\n".join(ad.get("defined", [])))
q("CFG_DEFINES_UNDEFINED", "\n".join(ad.get("undefined", [])))

# qemu
q("CFG_QEMU_BIN",     qe.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_MACHINE", qe.get("machine", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CPU",     qe.get("cpu", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_CONFIG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, qe.get("config", "")))
q("CFG_QEMU_VLEN",    qe.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_QEMU_TIMEOUT", qe.get("timeout", ""))

# vsim
q("CFG_VSIM_BIN",        vs.get("binary", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_VLEN",       vs.get("vlen", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_TARGET",     vs.get("target", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_MODELER",    vs.get("pipeline_modeler", ""))
q("CFG_VSIM_LOG_CYCLES", vs.get("log_cycles_default", ""))

# fpga
q("CFG_FPGA_HOST",        fp.get("host", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_PORT",        fp.get("port", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT",     fp.get("timeout", ""))
q("CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT",   abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("linker_script", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER", abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("sim_wrapper", "")))
q("CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG",  abspath_rel(libnn_root, fp.get("reset_flag_file", "")))

# limits
q("CFG_MAX_ROUNDS",       lm.get("max_rounds", ""))
q("CFG_VALIDATE_CACHE_H", lm.get("validate_cache_hours", ""))

# For convenience: record the resolved config path itself
q("CFG_CONFIG_PATH", config_path)
PYEOF
)"

unset _pc_script_dir _pc_config_path
